| R. | src/enclosure_family/variant_r/parameters.py owns current Variant R dimensions and tolerances; src/enclosure_family/datums.py owns the proven family coordinate frame | mm; design coordinates X left/right, Y front/rear with front=-Y, Z bottom/top; all accepted artifacts are in assembly coordinates with no additional transform | src/enclosure_family/variant_r/assembly.py owns Variant R seam/lower-material composition over an explicit VariantRFoundation; seam.py and bottom_ownership.py own the extracted geometry; the active leaf is a compatibility adapter for the serialized legacy foundation<br>the 33-stage historical ancestry is used only by the immutable accepted-base producer at commit 789cf7f; the active composer receives explicit foundation dependencies and legacy bindings are restored and serialized | cataloged producer scripts/generate_variant_r.py publishes the authoritative base plus complete attestation; the current validator publishes bucket, baffle, six protected sections and diagnostics; final immutable identities are committed in final-acceptance-evidence.json<br>the current validator and independent comparison adapter pass full-part validity/topology/bounds/mass, six protected sections, bidirectional material difference, interface/print diagnostics and STEP round trips | current Variant R only; driver/horn, fill, brace and later geometry correction remain separately scoped; Variant I imports no Variant R owner | high for exact current source/input/job/artifact identities and deterministic equivalence; physical print, gasket, leak and acoustic behavior remain unverified |
| I. | historic references inherit the same distributed removable-closure sources; future integral parameters have no owner | mm; historic artifacts use the same enclosure assembly coordinates; future open-bottom print transform is intent only | historic artifacts are bucket/baffle partition plus gasket/hardware; semantic future branch point is full_base before the nominal/clearance split; integral front/open bottom/hatch booleans do not exist<br>pre-split lightweight source still depends on the complete mutation chain; selected diagnostics mix lightweight/front-fill stage identity | only hash-bound historic full-perimeter bucket, baffle, assembly and diagnostics; no Variant I output root<br>historic diagnostics only; no independent Variant I validator | future Variant I independent owner<br>future open-bottom/hatch synthesis | high that selected artifacts are removable-front geometry; high that future I geometry is absent; low source/artifact freshness |
| tube. | single-oval-port owns current bore/base values; header/serviceable/absorber/rear-corner stages own route composition | mm; selected tube is positioned in enclosure design coordinates and contains six rigid solids | base bore/duct, header, serviceable tower, internal absorber and rear-corner routing through nested source mutation<br>route source delegates through single-oval/header/serviceable/absorber ancestry; current source values differ from historic diagnostics | hash-bound tube_reference.step and route_diagnostics.json; no Phase A regeneration<br>historic route diagnostics and geometric continuity assertions; no current enclosure-fit validation | future enclosure keepouts<br>net-volume accounting<br>service path<br>resonator interface | high artifact geometry metrics; medium historic 40 mm route semantics; low current source/artifact equivalence |
| resonator. | Rev C model/duct/robust modules own mechanical study; Rev D generator owns its wider-slot/calibration differences | mm; Rev C placement is in enclosure coordinates; Rev D assembly is in local coordinates; Rev D print layout includes explicit separated part transforms | D-squat body/duct/cavity/slot construction, assembly composition and separate print-layout placement<br>Rev D imports Rev C generator/model/duct rather than replacing them; the two revisions intentionally remain distinct | hash-bound Rev C placement STEP, Rev D assembly/layout STEP and Rev D diagnostics<br>Rev C verify.py and Rev D diagnostics; neither replaces physical acoustic measurement | future tube mechanical interface<br>future physical calibration task | high artifact metrics and revision distinction; medium CAD acoustic prediction; physical target validation explicitly unknown |
| horn. | params.py owns current legacy DE250/JMLC inputs; final_horn.py exposes API; features/horn.py owns recurrence/profile and exact revolved/thickened/NURBS construction | mm and degrees; local horn construction revolves an XZ profile about Axis.Z with nonnegative radius; no selected STEP or enclosure transform | JMLC meridian calculation, exact acoustic surface revolution, wall thickening and NURBS conversion; no enclosure integration<br>stable API calls feature builder; current default selects legacy recurrence while 2007 formula also exists | formula/source references only in selected input set; no selected current STEP, sidecar or image<br>Onshape-safe health signs documented: one solid, no boundary/non-manifold edges, no interference and no OFFSET_SURFACE entities | future horn parameter owner<br>driver mount<br>bracket clamp stack<br>enclosure package | high formula/source provenance; low fit to 190 mm enclosure because current mouth is 222.463 mm |

## Coordinate and construction contract

- units: mm<br>design_axes: X: left/right; selected enclosure exterior spans approximately -95 to +95<br>Y: front/rear; front exterior is near -95 and rear exterior near +115<br>Z: bottom/top; selected enclosure exterior spans approximately -95 to +95<br>selected_family_envelope_mm: 190.0<br>210.0<br>190.0<br>selected_family_wall_stack_mm: outer_skin: 2.0<br>sand_void: 3.0<br>inner_skin: 2.0<br>conflict: Repository-level legacy contract and params.py describe a 203 mm, 3/12/3 enclosure. Those values are not silently shared with this 190x210 family.

- Construction stages: the immutable producer reconstructs the accepted base at exact geometry commit 789cf7f and attests every loaded dependency<br>the current active compatibility adapter imports that attested base and supplies the explicit VariantRFoundation boundary<br>src/enclosure_family/variant_r/assembly.py composes the extracted seam and lower-material owners without Variant I flags<br>measurement, equivalence, STEP publication, provenance and evidence adapters consume geometry without owning it<br>the current release publishes complete parts, six protected sections, diagnostics and exact source/job/artifact identity

- Output/validation boundary: catalog_model: development-190x210-tongue-groove<br>output_root: build/sand_cube_190x210_internal_squat_absorber_rear_corners_parabolic_side_g1_simple_tongue_groove_baffle<br>producer: scripts/generate_variant_r.py<br>validator: experiments/sand_cube_190x210_internal_squat_absorber_rear_corners_parabolic_side_g1_simple_tongue_groove_baffle/validate_simple_tongue_groove_baffle.py<br>authoritative_input_sha256: 441cc122c0383da257b16e80c4b424096f33b267cc95cab9d1278fb05a43a784<br>producer_job_id: 20260724T083757-atomic-refactor-final2-producer-5f287edf8b<br>release_job_id: 20260724T102722-atomic-refactor-landing-restored-release-52bd80460c<br>strict_job_id: 20260724T105504-atomic-refactor-landing-strict-a9945e2f37<br>current_state: pass; ignored build artifacts are derived and their immutable hashes, jobs, producer/source identities, measurements and attestations are committed in the source-closure and final-acceptance records
